# VitaFlow

> VitaFlow is a blood test tracking app that turns lab reports into plain-language insights and tracks biomarker trends over time.

VitaFlow helps individuals understand their blood test results through clear, educational explanations. It is available on iOS and is designed for personal wellness — not medical diagnosis. Users can upload lab reports, track biomarker trends across visits, and receive AI-powered plain-language summaries.

## Main pages

- [Home](https://vitaflow-app.com/): Landing page with product overview, features, and App Store download.
- [Privacy Policy](https://vitaflow-app.com/privacy): How VitaFlow collects, stores, and protects user data.
- [Terms of Service](https://vitaflow-app.com/terms): Terms governing use of the VitaFlow app and website.
- [Lab Test Library](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers): Educational reference pages for all supported blood biomarkers.

## Biomarker reference pages

Each page explains what the biomarker measures, its normal reference range, what high or low values may indicate, and how it fits into overall health monitoring.

- [Albumin](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/albumin)
- [Alkaline Phosphatase (ALP)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/alkaline-phosphatase)
- [ALT (Alanine Aminotransferase)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/alt)
- [AST (Aspartate Aminotransferase)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/ast)
- [Bilirubin (Total)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/bilirubin-total)
- [BUN (Blood Urea Nitrogen)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/bun)
- [Calcium](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/calcium)
- [Cortisol](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/cortisol)
- [Creatinine](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/creatinine)
- [CRP (C-Reactive Protein)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/crp)
- [DHEA-S](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/dhea-s)
- [eGFR (Estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/egfr)
- [ESR (Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/esr)
- [Estradiol](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/estradiol)
- [Ferritin](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/ferritin)
- [Folate](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/folate)
- [Free T3](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/free-t3)
- [Free T4](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/free-t4)
- [GGT (Gamma-Glutamyl Transferase)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/ggt)
- [Glucose](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/glucose)
- [HbA1c](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/hba1c)
- [HDL Cholesterol](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/hdl-cholesterol)
- [Hematocrit](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/hematocrit)
- [Hemoglobin](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/hemoglobin)
- [Homocysteine](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/homocysteine)
- [Insulin](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/insulin)
- [LDL Cholesterol](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/ldl-cholesterol)
- [Lymphocytes](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/lymphocytes)
- [Magnesium](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/magnesium)
- [MCV (Mean Corpuscular Volume)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/mcv)
- [Neutrophils](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/neutrophils)
- [Platelets](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/platelets)
- [RBC (Red Blood Cell Count)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/rbc)
- [Serum Iron](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/serum-iron)
- [Testosterone](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/testosterone)
- [TIBC (Total Iron Binding Capacity)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/tibc)
- [Total Cholesterol](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/total-cholesterol)
- [Triglycerides](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/triglycerides)
- [TSH (Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/tsh)
- [Uric Acid](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/uric-acid)
- [Vitamin B12](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/vitamin-b12)
- [Vitamin D](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/vitamin-d)
- [WBC (White Blood Cell Count)](https://vitaflow-app.com/biomarkers/wbc)

## About

VitaFlow is built for personal wellness and education. It does not provide medical diagnosis or treatment recommendations. Users should consult a qualified healthcare professional for medical decisions.
